articles.title: Meta Glasses Need to Be Stylish to Succeed, Zuckerberg Says
articles.description: Mark Zuckerberg's latest obsession is fashion, which he sees as key to getting more people to wear Meta's AI glasses as more than tech gadgets.
articles.content:
Mark Zuckerberg wants Meta's latest AI glasses to be more than a gadget.
For the Meta CEO, the challenge isn't cramming more AI into a pair of frames — it's making glasses people actually want to wear.
During an interview with Feed Me creator Emily S... [1869 chars]

articles.title: "Horrible asf": FIFA World Cup anthem featuring TWICE's JIHYO sparks backlash as fans slam AI-heavy music video
articles.description: International Sports News: A new global anthem has been released for the road to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but the online chatter has quickly moved beyond the music. TWICE’s JIHY.
articles.content: TWICE's JIHYO joined RedOne's FIFA World Cup anthem "Follow Me", alongside French Montana, LUDMILLA and Adriana C. While fans widely praised the song and JIHYO's vocals, the music video drew heavy criticism for its AI-generated visuals. Many called t... [4516 chars]

articles.title: Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
articles.description: Google’s smart home AI will be able to recognize people in your Familiar Faces based on their clothing, and include detailed audio descriptions in video event notes.
articles.content: A new update for Google Home could make it less likely your smart home cameras mistake you for someone else, just because you’re facing away from the camera. Starting June 23rd, Google’s expanding its facial recognition feature so that people you’ve ... [1159 chars]

articles.title: AI stock slump raises the question if investors are just taking profits or getting very nervous
articles.description: Tech companies are spending big on AI, but investors might be getting nervous.
articles.content:
By ALEX VEIGA
Technology companies are spending big to incorporate artificial intelligence into their businesses and to build huge data centers. Investors who had jumped on the bandwagon appear to be having second thoughts.
